evals.prompt: I added a property to the HikeLog entity. How do I create a migration?
evals.expected_output: Provides dotnet ef migrations add <Name> command with both --project src/HikingLog.Infrastructure and --startup-project src/HikingLog.Api flags on a single line. Mentions PascalCase naming convention with a descriptive name (e.g. AddWeatherToHikeLog). Notes that dotnet build should pass before adding the migration.
assertions: Provides the dotnet ef migrations add command
Command includes --project src/HikingLog.Infrastructure
Command includes --startup-project src/HikingLog.Api
Mentions or demonstrates a PascalCase descriptive migration name
Mentions running dotnet build before adding the migration

evals.prompt: Update the database to the latest migration.
evals.expected_output: Provides dotnet ef database update with --project src/HikingLog.Infrastructure and --startup-project src/HikingLog.Api. Both flags present.
assertions: Provides the dotnet ef database update command
Command includes --project src/HikingLog.Infrastructure
Command includes --startup-project src/HikingLog.Api

evals.prompt: Which EF commands do I need to run after modifying the DbContext?
evals.expected_output: Describes the full workflow: (1) dotnet build to verify compilation, (2) dotnet ef migrations add <Name> with both project flags, (3) review the generated Up() and Down() methods, (4) dotnet ef database update with both flags, (5) dotnet test to confirm nothing broke. All commands include --project and --startup-project.
assertions: Mentions running dotnet build first
Provides dotnet ef migrations add with both --project and --startup-project flags
Mentions reviewing the generated Up() and/or Down() methods
Provides dotnet ef database update with both --project and --startup-project flags
Mentions running tests after applying the migration
All EF commands include both project flags

evals.prompt: I created a wrong migration that has not been applied yet. How do I remove it?
evals.expected_output: Provides dotnet ef migrations remove with --project src/HikingLog.Infrastructure and --startup-project src/HikingLog.Api. Includes explicit warning that this only works if the migration has NOT been applied to the database yet. If already applied, advises to add a new corrective migration instead.
assertions: Provides the dotnet ef migrations remove command
Command includes --project src/HikingLog.Infrastructure
Command includes --startup-project src/HikingLog.Api
Explicitly warns that remove only works when the migration has not been applied
Mentions adding a new corrective migration as the alternative when already applied

evals.prompt: I extended the Route entity with a GPX field. What are the next steps?
evals.expected_output: Describes: (1) run dotnet build first, (2) dotnet ef migrations add AddGpxFieldToRoute (descriptive name) with both project flags, (3) open the generated migration file and review Up/Down, (4) dotnet ef database update with both flags, (5) run tests. Migration naming convention (PascalCase, descriptive) mentioned.
assertions: Mentions running dotnet build before adding the migration
Provides dotnet ef migrations add with a descriptive PascalCase name (e.g. AddGpxFieldToRoute or similar)
Command includes --project src/HikingLog.Infrastructure
Command includes --startup-project src/HikingLog.Api
Mentions reviewing the generated migration file (Up/Down methods)
Provides dotnet ef database update with both project flags
Mentions running the build or tests after applying the migration
